name: Zendesk Sell CRM API
what_it_does: REST API for managing sales CRM data in Zendesk Sell including leads, contacts, deals, tasks, calls, emails, and pipeline stages with full CRUD and search capabilities.
use_cases: Automatically create leads from web form submissions and assign them to sales reps based on territory rules
Sync deal stage changes to external reporting systems or trigger downstream workflows
Enrich contact and company records with data from external sources such as LinkedIn or Clearbit
Generate pipeline reports by querying deals by stage, owner, and close date range
Automate task creation for follow-up actions when deals move to specific pipeline stages
not_for: Customer support ticket management — use the Zendesk Support API for that
Marketing automation or email campaign management at scale
Complex multi-object CRM workflows requiring Salesforce-level customization
best_when: You need to automate sales pipeline management, lead routing, or CRM data synchronization for teams already using Zendesk Sell.
avoid_when: Your team uses Zendesk Support but not Sell — these are separate products with separate APIs and data stores.

auth.notes: Supports OAuth 2.0 for user-delegated access and API tokens for server-to-server integrations. API tokens can be generated from the Sell admin settings. OAuth is recommended for multi-user applications. Scopes control access to read/write for each resource type. Token-based auth is straightforward and well-documented, making this one of the simpler CRM auth implementations.

pricing.paid_tiers: Sell Team ($19/agent/month), Sell Growth ($55/agent/month), Sell Professional ($115/agent/month). API access included in all paid plans.
pricing.notes: API access is included with all Zendesk Sell paid subscriptions. Some advanced features (custom fields, automation) require higher tiers. A 14-day free trial is available.

performance.rate_limits: 700 requests per minute per access token for most endpoints. Bulk endpoints have separate limits. Rate limit status is returned in X-RateLimit-Limit and X-RateLimit-Remaining headers. Exceeding limits returns 429 with a Retry-After header.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors are returned as JSON with a status code, error type, and descriptive message. Validation errors include field-level detail about which field failed and why. Error messages are generally clear and actionable. Rate limit errors include a Retry-After header. Overall one of the more agent-friendly error implementations in the CRM space.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No explicit idempotency keys, but the API is largely predictable. Upsert patterns can be implemented using search-then-create logic. Bulk create endpoints do not support idempotency — duplicate submissions create duplicate records.
known_agent_gotchas: Leads and Contacts are separate object types — a lead must be manually converted to a contact and company before it can be associated with a deal; agents that skip the conversion step will find deals cannot be linked to unconverted leads
Custom fields require fetching the field definitions separately to get field IDs before creating or updating records — hardcoding field names instead of IDs will cause validation errors

The search API uses a custom query syntax (not full-text search) and has significant limitations on filterable fields — agents should verify that their intended filter combinations are supported before building search-dependent logic
Webhooks for Sell are configured separately from Zendesk Support webhooks and require a different endpoint and authentication approach — do not assume Support webhook configuration applies to Sell

Bulk operations (bulk create, bulk update) have lower rate limits and size limits per batch — agents should chunk large sync operations and implement backoff logic even when individual call rates are within normal limits

security_score_components.security_notes: All traffic over TLS. OAuth 2.0 with scopes or API tokens. API tokens are per-user and can be revoked individually. OAuth scopes allow read-only access for reporting integrations. Zendesk maintains SOC 2 Type II certification. Webhook payloads include a secret token for validation.
